# Install and operate codex-autoupdate

Act as the installer. Perform the installation and verify it; do not ask the user to choose a Go binary directory or manually register launchd.

This software is for macOS. It requires Go, a logged-in administrator account, and ChatGPT Desktop. It installs one binary at `~/Library/Application Support/codex-autoupdate/codex-autoupdate` and registers the per-user LaunchAgent `com.tylergannon.codex-autoupdate`. It never uses `sudo`.

## Install or upgrade

Download the versioned installer, verify its published content hash, inspect it, and run it:

```sh
installer=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$installer"' EXIT
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tylergannon/codex-autoupdate/v0.1.1/install.sh -o "$installer"
printf '%s  %s\n' a6789cb1ae3e0d23df6fdd396424178ac031652e1f6223a2695e85f602306331 "$installer" | shasum -a 256 -c -
sed -n '1,220p' "$installer"
bash "$installer"
```

The same procedure upgrades an existing installation in place and refreshes its LaunchAgent.

To change the defaults, pass Cobra's global flags to the installer. For example:

```sh
bash "$installer" --idle-window 10m --poll-interval 30m
```

The default idle window is five minutes and the default update poll is fifteen minutes. A relocated ChatGPT application is supported with `--app-path /absolute/path/ChatGPT.app`.

## Verify and operate

```sh
binary="$HOME/Library/Application Support/codex-autoupdate/codex-autoupdate"
"$binary" --version
"$binary" status
"$binary" check --json
```

`check` is read-only. Logs are written under `~/Library/Logs/codex-autoupdate/`.

Useful foreground and removal commands:

```sh
"$binary" run --once
"$binary" uninstall
```

Uninstall stops the LaunchAgent and removes its plist and canonical binary. Logs, downloads, and failed-build quarantine markers are retained for diagnosis; remove `~/Library/Logs/codex-autoupdate/` and `~/Library/Caches/codex-autoupdate/` separately only when that history is no longer wanted.

## Update behavior and recovery

The watcher compares the installed `CFBundleVersion` with OpenAI's stable ChatGPT Desktop Sparkle appcast. It stages the full update, verifies the exact build, bundle identifier, OpenAI signing team, strict code signature, Gatekeeper acceptance, and host architecture, then waits until Desktop Codex rollout records show no active task for the configured window. It checks activity again before asking ChatGPT to quit normally and never force-kills the app.

If the replacement app-server does not become ready, the previous app is restored and relaunched. The failed build is quarantined at `~/Library/Caches/codex-autoupdate/failed-build-BUILD.json`, preventing repeated restarts for that build. A newer build remains eligible. Remove that marker only when deliberately retrying the same build.

The real end-to-end update proof is documented in `ephemeral/projects/codex-autoupdate/human-proof.md` and must be run from a separate Terminal when OpenAI advertises a build newer than the installed build.
